A sealed glyph machine transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs
by one fixed hidden rule.
The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za).
The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.

Probe log (input -> output):
  lus-mu-ro-ro-lus-gor-ti -> ro-gor-fen-ke-ro-fen-lus
  ke-mu-ro-za-mu-gor-gor -> ti-gor-fen-ke-gor-mu-ke
  ro-fen-za-ke-gor-mu-ti -> fen-za-mu-gor-ke-ti-lus
  ti-ke-gor-ke-ke-lus-ke -> lus-ti-ke-ro-ti-ti-ti

The machine now reads: ti-gor-mu-lus-gor-ke-ke
What does it print?
Write glyph strings dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

This SPOOL routine was correct until exactly one line was changed
(the planted bug):

1: IFZ C
2: SWAP B A
3: SWAP D B
4: ADD D 3
5: SUB A 5
6: ADD D 5

Each test starts the registers as shown; 'correct' is what the
original routine yields, 'buggy' is what the routine above yields.

T1: start A=0 B=4 C=0 D=2 | correct A=0 B=2 C=0 D=12 | buggy A=0 B=2 C=0 D=12 -> pass
T2: start A=2 B=6 C=0 D=3 | correct A=0 B=3 C=0 D=14 | buggy A=0 B=3 C=0 D=14 -> pass
T3: start A=1 B=6 C=8 D=6 | correct A=1 B=1 C=8 D=14 | buggy A=1 B=6 C=8 D=9 -> FAIL

Which line number holds the planted bug? (If replacing some other
single line would also make every test pass, that line number is
accepted too.)

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. swap first last

E2: fole varn fole varn => fole yeg fole yeg | shown wheel gives fole fole fole fole

rule could also make every evidence tape match, that number is
accepted too.)

non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A sealed glyph machine transforms any string of exactly 8 glyphs

  ti-lus-za-mu-lus-ke-ke-mu -> ro-za-lus-ro-gor-za-za-ro

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly ONE rule of this patchwheel was corrupted:

1. reverse all
2. every plim becomes zim
3. every quom becomes plim
4. reverse all

Work out the corrupted rule from the evidence, repair it, then
apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: nack zim plim ulm quom
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)

The 8 steps:
- Quenching: needs Kindlecall, Veiling, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=OPEN, fire-core=COLD.
- Glossing: needs Ashdraw, Coiling, ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=LIT; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Slagturn: needs Coiling, ash-vent=BOUND; then damper=OPEN, glaze-mark=SET.
- Raking: needs Quenching, ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=COLD; then fire-core=LIT, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Coiling: needs Kindlecall, damper=SHUT; then fire-core=LIT.
- Ashdraw: needs Kindlecall, Slagturn, fire-core=LIT; then damper=OPEN, ash-vent=FREE.
- Kindlecall: needs damper=SHUT; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Veiling: needs Ashdraw, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=SHUT.

Steps done so far, in order: Kindlecall, Coiling, Slagturn, Ashdraw, Veiling.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:

drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 12 steps:
- Glossing: needs Ashdraw, ash-vent=FREE; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- Veiling: needs Wadding, damper=SHUT, slip-ring=WHOLE; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Ashdraw: needs Charmark, Kindlecall, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=CRACKED; then soot-gate=RAISED.
- First Draw: needs Kindlecall, Veiling, Wadding, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=LOWERED; then fire-core=COLD.
- Tamping: needs Charmark, Wadding, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=LIT, slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Coiling: needs Emberfast, fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE, soot-gate=RAISED; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Slagturn: needs Ashdraw, First Draw, Veiling, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Kindlecall: needs Veiling, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Wadding: needs slip-ring=WHOLE; then soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Charmark: needs Kindlecall, ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=LIT; then damper=OPEN, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Quenching: needs Glossing, Wadding, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED; then soot-gate=RAISED, damper=SHUT.
- Emberfast: needs Ashdraw, First Draw, Glossing, ash-vent=BOUND, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=SET.

Steps done so far, in order: Wadding, Veiling, Kindlecall, Charmark, First Draw, Ashdraw, Tamping, Glossing, Slagturn, Emberfast, Quenching.

A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

4. every nack becomes ruv

apply the CORRECTED wheel to this fresh input: tosk tosk dree dree ruv
Give the output tape as tokens separated by single spaces.

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

T2: start A=3 B=0 C=4 D=0 | correct A=3 B=0 C=4 D=0 | buggy A=3 B=0 C=4 D=8 -> FAIL

Which line number holds the planted bug? (If replacing some other
single line would also make every test pass, that line number is
accepted too.)

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)

The 12 steps:
- Emberfast: needs Cold Turning, Tamping, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Glossing: needs Emberfast, Tamping, Wadding, ash-vent=FREE; then slip-ring=CRACKED, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Veiling: needs Ashdraw, Glossing, Tamping, glaze-mark=SET, soot-gate=RAISED; then fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Kindlecall: needs Ashdraw, damper=OPEN; then ash-vent=BOUND, fire-core=LIT.
- Scrying: needs Tamping, ash-vent=BOUND, damper=OPEN, soot-gate=LOWERED; then ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=SET.
- Wadding: needs Emberfast, ash-vent=BOUND; then damper=OPEN, glaze-mark=SET.
- Cold Turning: needs ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- First Draw: needs Cold Turning, Kindlecall, Tamping, slip-ring=CRACKED; then ash-vent=BOUND.
- Tamping: needs Cold Turning, fire-core=COLD; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Quenching: needs Cold Turning, Scrying, soot-gate=LOWERED; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Sealing: needs Scrying, Wadding, ash-vent=FREE, glaze-mark=SET; then fire-core=COLD, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Ashdraw: needs Tamping, soot-gate=LOWERED; then ash-vent=FREE.

Steps done so far, in order: Cold Turning, Tamping, Emberfast, Wadding, Ashdraw, Glossing, Kindlecall, Veiling, First Draw, Scrying.

A kiln-firing procedure is documented below.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.

Flags at the start:
- fire-core: COLD (values LIT/COLD)
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)

The 12 steps:
- Raking: needs Slagturn, ash-vent=BOUND; then slip-ring=CRACKED, damper=OPEN.
- Glossing: needs Raking, Slagturn, Tamping, damper=OPEN, glaze-mark=UNSET; then damper=SHUT, fire-core=COLD.
- Kindlecall: needs Sealing, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then ash-vent=FREE, fire-core=LIT.
- Sealing: needs First Draw, Raking, Slagturn, fire-core=COLD, soot-gate=RAISED; then damper=OPEN, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Quenching: needs Kindlecall, ash-vent=FREE, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT; then soot-gate=RAISED, glaze-mark=SET.
- Charmark: needs Ashdraw, Tamping, damper=SHUT, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET; then glaze-mark=UNSET, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Scrying: needs Raking, damper=OPEN, fire-core=COLD; then glaze-mark=SET, ash-vent=BOUND.
- Slagturn: needs damper=SHUT; then fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET.
- Tamping: needs Raking, damper=SHUT, glaze-mark=SET; then glaze-mark=UNSET, damper=OPEN.
- Veiling: needs Glossing, soot-gate=LOWERED; then glaze-mark=SET, slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Ashdraw: needs Sealing, ash-vent=FREE, damper=OPEN, glaze-mark=SET; then damper=SHUT.
- First Draw: needs Raking, Slagturn, damper=OPEN, fire-core=LIT, glaze-mark=SET; then ash-vent=FREE, damper=SHUT.

Steps done so far, in order: Slagturn, Raking, First Draw, Tamping, Glossing, Sealing, Scrying, Kindlecall, Veiling, Quenching.

Exactly one step is legal to do next. Which one? Answer with the step name only.

You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- soot-gate: LOWERED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- slip-ring: CRACKED (values WHOLE/CRACKED)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- ash-vent: BOUND (values FREE/BOUND)
- damper: SHUT (values OPEN/SHUT)

- Tamping: needs Glossing, damper=SHUT; then slip-ring=WHOLE.

- Raking: needs Glossing, damper=OPEN; then soot-gate=LOWERED, damper=SHUT.
- First Draw: needs fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED; then slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Sealing: needs Cold Turning, fire-core=COLD; then ash-vent=FREE.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: ti-ke-za-za-ti-ke-za. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.
TARGET: ti-ke-za-za-ti-ke-za. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.
PROBE za-ke-ro-mu-ti-fen-gor
The gate prints: gor-ke-ti-mu-ro-ke-za. Probes left: 7. Turns left: 17.
PROBE ke-ro-mu-ti-fen-gor-za

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.

RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail

system_prompt:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: The kiln is prepared. 0 of 10 steps done. Turn budget: 18.

messages.content:
You are performing a kiln-firing procedure, one action per turn.
- Steps may be done in any order, each at most once.
- A step is legal only if every 'needs' item holds right then: a bare name is a step that must already be done; flag=WORD means that flag currently reads WORD.
- Doing a step immediately sets each flag listed after 'then'.
- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.
- Goal: complete all 10 steps within 18 turns.

Flags at the start:
- ash-vent: FREE (values FREE/BOUND)
- glaze-mark: SET (values SET/UNSET)
- soot-gate: RAISED (values RAISED/LOWERED)
- fire-core: LIT (values LIT/COLD)
- slip-ring: WHOLE (values WHOLE/CRACKED)

The 10 steps:
- Veiling: needs Quenching, Wadding, glaze-mark=SET; then glaze-mark=UNSET.
- Raking: needs Kindlecall, fire-core=COLD; then fire-core=LIT, soot-gate=LOWERED.
- Ashdraw: needs Wadding, glaze-mark=UNSET, slip-ring=CRACKED; then fire-core=COLD, slip-ring=WHOLE.
- Scrying: needs Raking, Sealing, glaze-mark=SET; then soot-gate=LOWERED, slip-ring=CRACKED.
- Wadding: needs Kindlecall, Raking, ash-vent=FREE; then ash-vent=BOUND, soot-gate=RAISED.
- Sealing: needs Kindlecall, soot-gate=RAISED; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Glossing: needs Coiling, Quenching, slip-ring=WHOLE; then ash-vent=FREE.
- Quenching: needs Coiling, Kindlecall, ash-vent=BOUND, glaze-mark=UNSET; then fire-core=COLD, glaze-mark=SET.
- Kindlecall: needs fire-core=LIT, slip-ring=WHOLE; then fire-core=COLD.
- Coiling: needs Kindlecall, ash-vent=FREE; then glaze-mark=UNSET, ash-vent=BOUND.

Action grammar: DO <StepName>

initial_observation: TARGET: ke-lus-lus-za-fen-ke-ti. The gate accepts strings of exactly 7 glyphs. Probes left: 8. Turns left: 18.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 7 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule chains TWO steps in order (the second step reads the result of the first); each step is one of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

messages.content:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are exploring an underground burrow network of connected chambers.
Each turn you see only your current chamber and its exit directions; where an exit leads stays unknown until you walk through it, so remember what you discover.
Corridors are consistent: the way back is always the exact reverse direction (north-south, east-west, up-down).
Reach the target chamber within 6 moves; entering it ends the trek at once. Every action costs one move, even an invalid one.
Action grammar: GO <direction>   (directions: north, south, east, west, up, down)
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

- An illegal, repeated, unknown, or malformed action changes nothing and still costs the turn.
- Flag values are never shown after the start; track them yourself.

initial_observation: TARGET: gor-fen-mu-ti. The gate accepts strings of exactly 4 glyphs. Probes left: 4. Turns left: 6.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 4 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET

SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

messages.content:
You are repairing a patchwheel at the tapeworks bench.
A patchwheel is an ordered list of rewrite rules run on a token tape:
rule 1 first on the whole tape, each later rule on the result. Forms:
every A becomes B = every token A turns into B
swap first last = exchange the first and last tokens
reverse all = reverse the tape
rotate left = the first token moves to the end
rotate right = the last token moves to the front
drop after A = left to right, delete the token right after each A
  (a deleted token is not scanned)
drop before A = left to right, delete the kept token right before
  each A (an A can delete an earlier A)
double A = every A turns into A A

Exactly one rule of the shown wheel was corrupted. Evidence lists each input tape and the output the ORIGINAL wheel gave. Repair the wheel so every evidence tape matches, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
RULE <k>: <rule text> — replace rule k (e.g. RULE 2: every varn becomes ruv)
RUN — recheck all evidence tapes and report match/mismatch
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

initial_observation: TARGET: lus-ti-za-ti-mu. The gate accepts strings of exactly 5 glyphs. Probes left: 5. Turns left: 10.

messages.content:
You stand before a sealed glyph gate. It transforms any string of exactly 5 glyphs by one fixed hidden rule. The glyph alphabet, in cycle order: za, ke, ro, mu, ti, fen, gor, lus (after lus the cycle wraps back to za). The hidden rule is ONE of: (a) the whole string is reversed; (b) the glyphs at two secret fixed positions trade places; (c) one secret fixed position is overwritten with one secret fixed glyph; (d) every glyph steps forward the same secret count along the alphabet cycle; (e) if the first glyph is in a secret pair of glyphs the whole string rotates one step left, otherwise one step right.
Goal: feed the gate an input that it transforms into the TARGET string.
Actions (one per turn):
  PROBE <glyph-string>  - spend one probe; the gate prints the transformed string
  OPEN <glyph-string>   - final commit; you succeed only if the gate's output equals the TARGET
Glyph strings are dash-joined, e.g. za-ke-ro. Probes are limited; OPEN ends the episode.
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.

system_prompt:
You are repairing a routine for the loom-house's pattern engine.
SPOOL is a tiny register language. Four registers A, B, C, D hold
non-negative integers. One instruction per line, executed top to bottom:
ADD r n = add n to register r
SUB r n = subtract n from r (floor at 0)
COPY r s = set r to the current value of s
SWAP r s = exchange the values of r and s
ZERO r = set r to 0
IFZ r = if r is 0, skip the next line

Exactly one line of the routine was changed from the correct version. Each test starts the registers as shown and lists the finals the CORRECT routine yields. Repair the routine so every test passes, then RUN to confirm.
Actions (one per turn):
PATCH <line#> <instruction> — replace that line (e.g. PATCH 3 ADD B 2)
RUN — rerun all tests and report pass/fail
Reply with exactly one action line and nothing else.
